symptoms.symptom_name: Ischaemic rest pain relieved by dependency, worsened by elevation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rest pain relieved by hanging the foot down is highly specific for critical arterial insufficiency (gravity-assisted perfusion).
Neuropathic pain is position-independent; dependency-relief points to an arterial, not neuropathic, cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dry gangrene of the distal great toe (black, hard, mummified tissue)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Distal dry gangrene is end-stage arterial tissue loss (Fontaine IV / Rutherford 5-6).

symptoms.symptom_name: Non-healing punched-out ischaemic ulcer on the lateral malleolus

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Arterial ulcers are punched-out, painful and non-healing on the distal/lateral limb, distinct from sloping venous ulcers.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pale, perishingly cold, shiny foot with dependent rubor

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Coldness, pallor, shiny hairless skin and dependent rubor are hallmarks of chronic severe hypoperfusion.
DVT produces a warm, swollen, engorged limb — the opposite of a cold, pale, shiny foot.

symptoms.symptom_name: Prior intermittent claudication escalating to rest pain over two weeks

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The claudication-to-rest-pain progression is the natural history of PAD advancing to a limb-threatening stage.
A gradual weeks-long progression argues against a sudden embolic occlusion, though acute-on-chronic occlusion remains possible.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absent dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulses; diminished popliteal pulse

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absent distal pulses confirm severe arterial occlusive disease; a sealed clinician examination finding not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Capillary refill time severely delayed (>4 seconds)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Prolonged capillary refill reflects poor distal perfusion; a clinician-observed sign.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive Buerger's test (elevation pallor with dependent rubor)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A positive Buerger's test indicates severe arterial insufficiency; a clinician bedside manoeuvre not performable via telehealth.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Severity of ischaemia under-appreciated; ABI <0.40 denotes critical ischaemia requiring urgent vascular referral.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: ABI <0.40 is diagnostic of critical arterial insufficiency; a vascular-lab/clinician measurement, never available via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, rigors or signs of wet/infected gangrene

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever, warmth, swelling, purulence and malodour indicates dry (non-infected) gangrene at present; must be safety-netted for conversion.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Ischaemic rest pain relieved by dependency + tissue loss + absent distal pulses + ABI <0.40 together define severe, limb-threatening critical limb ischaemia.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Limb-threatening ischaemia with tissue loss requires same-day specialist assessment for revascularisation (angioplasty/bypass) or amputation planning.
Quantifies severity of arterial insufficiency; performed in the in-person/hospital setting, not via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Arterial duplex ultrasound / CT or MR angiography
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Defines the level and extent of arterial occlusion to plan revascularisation; hospital-based imaging.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: FBE, CRP and wound swab if infection suspected
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To detect conversion to wet/infected gangrene; only if clinical signs of infection develop.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical home antibiotics for the black toe
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Dry gangrene without infection does not need antibiotics; the AI must not initiate antibiotics remotely — this is a vascular emergency, not a wound-infection triage.
